tree.title: What should I eat tonight?
tree.description: A decision tree to help you figure out dinner.

branch.content: Are you cooking or ordering in?
How much time do you have?
Cooking solo or feeding others?
Scrambled eggs with toast, or pasta with jarred sauce. Both are fast, filling, and barely any washing up.
Tacos. Everyone builds their own, you can use pre-cooked protein, and it is on the table in 15 minutes.
Do you eat meat?
Pan-seared chicken thighs with roasted vegetables. Season well, high heat, done in 40 minutes with minimal effort.
Stir-fried tofu with vegetables over rice. Press the tofu first for crispiness. Ready in 35 minutes and genuinely satisfying.
Feeling adventurous?
branch.fork: Yes, let us go all out

branch.content: Make a curry from scratch. Toast whole spices, build a proper base, let it simmer low and slow. The smell alone is worth it.
Slow-roasted chicken with potatoes and garlic. Put it in the oven and forget about it for 90 minutes. Classic for a reason.
What are you in the mood for?
Are you going for comfort or quality?
A big chain is fine for this. Order extra and have leftovers for tomorrow. No shame in it.
Find the highest-rated independent pizzeria near you and go for their signature pie. Worth the wait.
Which kind?
Check reviews carefully and go for a place that is busy. Fresh fish matters. Splurge a little if you can.
Dumplings, fried rice, and General Tso. Order more than you think you need. The classics are classics for a reason.
Pad Thai or green curry. Ask for the real spice level, not the tourist version. Have jasmine rice on the side.
Do you want a sit-down place or delivery?
branch.fork: I will go pick it up or eat out

branch.content: Burritos travel better than tacos. Order guacamole even if it costs extra. You know you want it.
A proper taqueria beats delivery every time. Find one with handmade tortillas if you can.
Fast food or a proper burger joint?
branch.fork: Fast food, I am not fussy
A real burger from a real place

branch.content: Go for it. No judgment. Get the fries and a milkshake while you are at it.
Find a local burger spot with good reviews. A smash burger with proper cheese and a brioche bun beats everything.
